messages.content:
Build an example application circuit for **MAX2870**.

## Component profile
{
  "slug": "MAX2870_-_23-5MHZ_TO_6000MHZ_FRACTIONAL_INTEGER-N_SYNTHESIZER_VCO_-_MAX2870",
  "part_number": "MAX2870",
  "name": "MAX2870",
  "description": "23.5MHz to 6000MHz Fractional/Integer-N Synthesizer/VCO with integrated VCOs, dual differential RF outputs, 4-wire SPI, 32-pin TQFN",
  "manufacturer": "Maxim Integrated",
  "package_types": [
    "TQFN-32-EP"
  ],
  "supply_voltage_range": "3.0\u20133.6V",
  "frequency_range": "23.5MHz\u20136000MHz",
  "designator": "U",
  "absolute_max": {
    "vdd": "3.9V (VCC_ to GND_)",
    "all_other_pins": "VCC_ + 0.3V to GND_ \u2212 0.3V",
    "operating_temp": "\u221240\u00b0C to +85\u00b0C",
    "junction_temp": "+150\u00b0C",
    "storage_temp": "\u221265\u00b0C to +150\u00b0C",
    "lead_temp_soldering_10s": "+300\u00b0C",
    "reflow_temp": "+260\u00b0C"
  },
  "key_performance": {
    "pll_modes": "Integer-N and Fractional-N",
    "vco_range_fundamental": "3000MHz to 6000MHz",
    "output_dividers": "1/2/4/8/16/32/64/128 \u2014 extends range down to 23.5MHz",
    "pfd_frequency_integer_n": "105MHz max",
    "pfd_frequency_frac_n": "50MHz max",
    "reference_input_range": "10MHz to 200MHz (100MHz max with \u00d72 doubler enabled)",
    "output_power_range": "\u22124dBm to +5dBm (3dB steps), each output independently programmable",
    "outputs": "Dual differential open-collector (RFOUTA\u00b1, RFOUTB\u00b1); RFOUTB can be set to VCO fundamental via BDIV bit",
    "supply": "3.0V to 3.6V",
    "logic_compatibility": "1.8V SPI (VIL < 0.4V, VIH > 1.5V)",
    "supply_current_max": "180mA (both channels, max output power, both divide-by-2)",
    "supply_current_sleep": "1mA",
    "interface": "4-wire SPI: CLK, DATA, LE (write); MUX_OUT (readback of Register 6)",
    "registers": "6 write-only + 1 read-only (Reg 6) 32-bit registers",
    "vco_phase_noise_3ghz": "\u221283.5dBc/Hz @ 10kHz, \u2212111dBc/Hz @ 100kHz, \u2212136dBc/Hz @ 1MHz",
    "integrated_rms_jitter": "0.25ps typical",
    "cp_current_range": "0.32mA to 5.12mA (RSET = 5.1k\u03a9)"
  },
  "pins": [
    {
      "number": "1",
      "name": "CLK",
      "type": "input",
      "description": "Serial Clock Input. Data is latched into the 32-bit shift register on the rising edge of CLK.",
      "active": null,
      "internal_pull": null,
      "requirements": "Connect to SPI master clock. Min CLK period 50ns (20MHz max). Meets 1.8V logic thresholds. tDS=25ns setup, tDH=25ns hold on DATA.",
      "ambiguous": false,
      "datasheet_page": 10
    },
    {
      "number": "2",
      "name": "DATA",
      "type": "input",
      "description": "Serial Data Input. Data loaded MSB first. The 3 LSBs identify the register address.",
      "active": null,
      "internal_pull": null,
      "requirements": "Connect to SPI master MOSI. Data must be stable tDS=25ns before and tDH=25ns after rising CLK edge.",
      "ambiguous": false,
      "datasheet_page": 10
    },
    {
      "number": "3",
      "name": "LE",
      "type": "input",
      "description": "Load Enable Input. Rising edge of LE latches the shift register contents into the selected register.",
      "active": "high",
      "internal_pull": null,
      "requirements": "Connect to SPI CS (active-high latch). tLES=20ns setup before rising edge, tLEH=10ns hold after. Minimum pulse-width high tLEW=20ns.",
      "ambiguous": false,
      "datasheet_page": 10
    },
    {
      "number": "4",
      "name": "CE",
      "type": "input",
      "description": "Chip Enable. Logic-low powers down the device; charge pump becomes high impedance. In low-power mode all blocks except SPI are off.",
      "active": "low",
      "internal_pull": null,
      "requirements": "Tie high (3.0\u20133.6V or VCC_DIG) for normal operation. Drive low to enter shutdown. After CE returns high, wait \u226520ms before programming registers.",
      "ambiguous": false,
      "datasheet_page": 10
    },
    {
      "number": "5",
      "name": "SW",
      "type": "passive",
      "description": "Fast-Lock Switch. Connect to loop filter when using fast-lock mode. No connect in normal mode.",
      "active": null,
      "internal_pull": null,
      "requirements": "Leave unconnected (NC) in normal operation. For fast-lock: connect to the mid-point of the split loop-filter resistor (R2A/R2B). Set CDM=01 in Register 3.",
      "ambiguous": false,
      "datasheet_page": 10
    },
    {
      "number": "6",
      "name": "VCC_CP",
      "type": "power",
      "description": "Power Supply for Charge Pump.",
      "active": null,
      "internal_pull": null,
      "requirements": "Connect to 3.0\u20133.6V. Star routing from central VCC node recommended. Place 100nF + 1\u00b5F decoupling caps as close as possible to pin. One via per cap to ground plane.",
      "ambiguous": false,
      "datasheet_page": 10
    },
    {
      "number": "7",
      "name": "CP_OUT",
      "type": "output",
      "description": "Charge-Pump Output. Current-output node that drives the external loop filter.",
      "active": null,
      "internal_pull": null,
      "requirements": "Connect to the input of the loop filter network (C3 to GND, then R3, then R2A node which connects to TUNE). Keep trace short. CP can be three-stated by TRI bit (Reg2 bit4).",
      "ambiguous": false,
      "datasheet_page": 10
    },
    {
      "number": "8",
      "name": "GND_CP",
      "type": "gnd",
      "description": "Ground for Charge Pump.",
      "active": null,
      "internal_pull": null,
      "requirements": "Connect to PCB board ground plane. Do NOT connect directly to the exposed paddle (EP). Route each GND_ pin separately to the ground plane.",
      "ambiguous": false,
      "datasheet_page": 10
    },
    {
      "number": "9",
      "name": "GND_PLL",
      "type": "gnd",
      "description": "Ground for PLL.",
      "active": null,
      "internal_pull": null,
      "requirements": "Connect to main PCB board ground plane. Do NOT connect directly to the exposed paddle (EP). Route each GND_ pin separately to the ground plane.",
      "ambiguous": false,
      "datasheet_page": 10
    },
    {
      "number": "10",
      "name": "VCC_PLL",
      "type": "power",
      "description": "Power Supply for PLL.",
      "active": null,
      "internal_pull": null,
      "requirements": "Connect to 3.0\u20133.6V. Datasheet recommends three separate LDOs for VCC_RF, VCC_DIG, and VCC_PLL for best performance. Place 100nF + 1\u00b5F decoupling caps close to pin.",
      "ambiguous": false,
      "datasheet_page": 10
    },
    {
      "number": "11",
      "name": "GND_RF",
      "type": "gnd",
      "description": "Ground for RF Outputs.",
      "active": null,
      "internal_pull": null,
      "requirements": "Connect to PCB board ground plane. Do NOT connect directly to the exposed paddle (EP). Route each GND_ pin separately to the ground plane.",
      "ambiguous": false,
      "datasheet_page": 10
    },
    {
      "number": "12",
      "name": "RFOUTA_P",
      "type": "output",
      "description": "Open Collector Positive RF Output A. Differential positive output. Fundamental VCO \u00f7 DIVA.",
      "active": null,
      "internal_pull": null,
      "requirements": "Bias via 27nH RF choke from VCC_RF for single-ended use, or via RF choke/50\u03a9 to supply for differential. Enable via RFA_EN bit (Reg4 bit5) and RFOUT_EN pin. If unused: connect 27nH choke to VCC_RF + 50\u03a9 termination to GND.",
      "ambiguous": false,
      "datasheet_page": 10
    },
    {
      "number": "13",
      "name": "RFOUTA_N",
      "type": "output",
      "description": "Open Collector Negative RF Output A. Differential negative output.",
      "active": null,
      "internal_pull": null,
      "requirements": "Bias via 27nH RF choke from VCC_RF. If unused: connect 27nH choke to VCC_RF + 50\u03a9 termination to GND.",
      "ambiguous": false,
      "datasheet_page": 11
    },
    {
      "number": "14",
      "name": "RFOUTB_P",
      "type": "output",
      "description": "Open Collector Positive RF Output B. Differential positive output. BDIV=0: same frequency as RFOUTA; BDIV=1: VCO fundamental frequency.",
      "active": null,
      "internal_pull": null,
      "requirements": "Bias via 27nH RF choke from VCC_RF. Enable via RFB_EN bit (Reg4 bit8). If unused: connect 27nH choke to VCC_RF + 50\u03a9 termination to GND.",
      "ambiguous": false,
      "datasheet_page": 11
    },
    {
      "number": "15",
      "name": "RFOUTB_N",
      "type": "output",
      "description": "Open Collector Negative RF Output B. Differential negative output.",
      "active": null,
      "internal_pull": null,
      "requirements": "Bias via 27nH RF choke from VCC_RF. If unused: connect 27nH choke to VCC_RF + 50\u03a9 termination to GND.",
      "ambiguous": false,
      "datasheet_page": 11
    },
    {
      "number": "16",
      "name": "VCC_RF",
      "type": "power",
      "description": "Power Supply for RF Output and Dividers. Also serves as DC bias supply for open-collector RF outputs via 27nH chokes.",
      "active": null,
      "internal_pull": null,
      "requirements": "Connect to 3.0\u20133.6V. Separate LDO recommended for best RF output performance. Place 100nF + 1\u00b5F decoupling caps close to pin. The 27nH RF chokes on each output pin connect back to this supply.",
      "ambiguous": false,
      "datasheet_page": 11
    },
    {
      "number": "17",
      "name": "VCC_VCO",
      "type": "power",
      "description": "VCO Power Supply.",
      "active": null,
      "internal_pull": null,
      "requirements": "Connect to 3.0\u20133.6V. Decoupling capacitors must connect to the analog ground plane (not digital ground plane) for best phase noise. Use 100nF + 1\u00b5F.",
      "ambiguous": false,
      "datasheet_page": 11
    },
    {
      "number": "18",
      "name": "GND_VCO",
      "type": "gnd",
      "description": "Ground for VCO.",
      "active": null,
      "internal_pull": null,
      "requirements": "Connect to main PCB board ground plane. Do NOT connect directly to the exposed paddle (EP). Route each GND_ pin separately to the ground plane.",
      "ambiguous": false,
      "datasheet_page": 11
    },
    {
      "number": "19",
      "name": "NOISE_FILT",
      "type": "passive",
      "description": "VCO Noise Decoupling. Internal VCO bias node that must be externally decoupled.",
      "active": null,
      "internal_pull": null,
      "requirements": "Place a 1\u00b5F capacitor from this pin directly to GND. Keep trace as short as possible. Do NOT omit \u2014 leaving floating degrades VCO phase noise significantly.",
      "ambiguous": false,
      "datasheet_page": 11
    },
    {
      "number": "20",
      "name": "TUNE",
      "type": "input",
      "description": "VCO Control Voltage Input. Driven by the output of the external PLL loop filter. Compliance range: 0.5V to 2.5V.",
      "active": null,
      "internal_pull": null,
      "requirements": "Connect to the output node of the loop filter network (not directly to CP_OUT). The 3-bit internal TUNE ADC monitors this voltage for VCO autoselect; valid range 0.5\u20132.5V for in-lock status.",
      "ambiguous": false,
      "datasheet_page": 11
    },
    {
      "number": "21",
      "name": "GND_TUNE",
      "type": "gnd",
      "description": "Ground for VCO Control Input.",
      "active": null,
      "internal_pull": null,
      "requirements": "Connect to main PCB board ground plane. Do NOT connect directly to the exposed paddle (EP). Route each GND_ pin separately to the ground plane.",
      "ambiguous": false,
      "datasheet_page": 11
    },
    {
      "number": "22",
      "name": "RSET",
      "type": "passive",
      "description": "Charge-Pump Current Range Input. External resistor to GND sets the minimum CP current: ICP = 1.63V/RSET \u00d7 (1 + CP[3:0]).",
      "active": null,
      "internal_pull": null,
      "requirements": "Connect a resistor between 2.7k\u03a9 and 10k\u03a9 from this pin to GND. Typical: 5.1k\u03a9 \u2192 ICP_min=0.32mA (CP=0000), ICP_max=5.12mA (CP=1111). Do NOT leave floating or tie to GND.",
      "ambiguous": false,
      "datasheet_page": 11
    },
    {
      "number": "23",
      "name": "BIAS_FILT",
      "type": "passive",
      "description": "VCO Bias Decoupling. Internal VCO bias filter node.",
      "active": null,
      "internal_pull": null,
      "requirements": "Place a 1\u00b5F capacitor from this pin directly to GND. Keep trace as short as possible. Do NOT omit.",
      "ambiguous": false,
      "datasheet_page": 11
    },
    {
      "number": "24",
      "name": "REG",
      "type": "passive",
      "description": "Reference Voltage Compensation. Internal bandgap/reference decoupling node.",
      "active": null,
      "internal_pull": null,
      "requirements": "Place a 1\u00b5F capacitor from this pin directly to GND. Keep trace as short as possible. Do NOT omit.",
      "ambiguous": false,
      "datasheet_page": 11
    },
    {
      "number": "25",
      "name": "LD",
      "type": "output",
      "description": "Lock Detect Output. Behaviour set by LD[1:0] bits (Reg5 bits 23:22): 01=digital lock detect, 10=analog lock detect (open-drain), 00=always low, 11=always high.",
      "active": "high",
      "internal_pull": null,
      "requirements": "For digital lock detect (LD=01): connect to MCU GPIO. VOH = VCC\u22120.4V at 0.3mA source, VOL = 0.4V at 0.3mA sink. For analog lock detect (LD=10): output is open-drain, add 10k\u03a9 pull-up to VCC.",
      "ambiguous": false,
      "datasheet_page": 11
    },
    {
      "number": "26",
      "name": "RFOUT_EN",
      "type": "input",
      "description": "RF Output Enable (hardware). Logic-low disables both RFOUTA and RFOUTB regardless of register settings.",
      "active": "high",
      "internal_pull": null,
      "requirements": "Pull high for normal RF output operation. Can be driven low by MCU to mute both outputs. Per-output software control via RFA_EN (Reg4 bit5) and RFB_EN (Reg4 bit8). Keep low during the register init sequence, then pull high after second programming pass.",
      "ambiguous": false,
      "datasheet_page": 11
    },
    {
      "number": "27",
      "name": "GND_DIG",
      "type": "gnd",
      "description": "Ground for Digital Circuitry.",
      "active": null,
      "internal_pull": null,
      "requirements": "Connect to main PCB board ground plane. Do NOT connect directly to the exposed paddle (EP). Route each GND_ pin separately to the ground plane.",
      "ambiguous": false,
      "datasheet_page": 11
    },
    {
      "number": "28",
      "name": "VCC_DIG",
      "type": "power",
      "description": "Power Supply for Digital Circuitry. Also provides the internal bias level VCC_DIG/2 for the REF_IN input.",
      "active": null,
      "internal_pull": null,
      "requirements": "Connect to 3.0\u20133.6V. Place 100nF + 1\u00b5F decoupling caps close to pin.",
      "ambiguous": false,
      "datasheet_page": 11
    },
    {
      "number": "29",
      "name": "REF_IN",
      "type": "input",
      "description": "Reference Frequency Input. High-impedance CMOS inverter with internal bias at VCC_DIG/2. Input range 10\u2013200MHz (or 10\u2013100MHz with reference doubler DBR=1). In shutdown, input is high-impedance.",
      "active": null,
      "internal_pull": null,
      "requirements": "Must be AC-coupled (internal bias at VCC_DIG/2 will be disturbed by DC coupling). Input sensitivity: 0.7\u00d7VCC_DIG Vpp minimum. Input capacitance 2pF. Input current \u00b160\u00b5A.",
      "ambiguous": false,
      "datasheet_page": 11
    },
    {
      "number": "30",
      "name": "MUX_OUT",
      "type": "output",
      "description": "Multiplexed Output / Serial Data Out. Function selected by MUX[3:0] (Reg2 bits 28:26 + Reg5 bit18 MSB). Options: high-Z, D_VDD, D_GND, R-divider output, N-divider/2, analog lock detect, digital lock detect, or Register-6 readback (MUX=1100).",
      "active": null,
      "internal_pull": null,
      "requirements": "Connect to MCU GPIO for lock detect or status monitoring. For Reg6 readback: set MUX=1100, then issue SPI read sequence per datasheet Figure 2. VOH=VCC\u22120.4V at 0.3mA, VOL=0.4V at 0.3mA. tMS=10ns setup, tMH=10ns hold.",
      "ambiguous": false,
      "datasheet_page": 11
    },
    {
      "number": "31",
      "name": "GND_SD",
      "type": "gnd",
      "description": "Ground for Sigma-Delta Modulator.",
      "active": null,
      "internal_pull": null,
      "requirements": "Connect to main PCB board ground plane. Do NOT connect directly to the exposed paddle (EP). Route each GND_ pin separately to the ground plane.",
      "ambiguous": false,
      "datasheet_page": 11
    },
    {
      "number": "32",
      "name": "VCC_SD",
      "type": "power",
      "description": "Power Supply for Sigma-Delta Modulator.",
      "active": null,
      "internal_pull": null,
      "requirements": "Connect to 3.0\u20133.6V. Place 100nF + 1\u00b5F decoupling caps close to pin.",
      "ambiguous": false,
      "datasheet_page": 11
    },
    {
      "number": "EP",
      "name": "EP",
      "type": "gnd",
      "description": "Exposed Pad. Thermal and ground pad on the bottom of the package. Must be soldered to PCB ground plane for proper electrical and thermal performance.",
      "active": null,
      "internal_pull": null,
      "requirements": "Solder to PCB ground plane using multiple vias (minimum 9 vias in 3\u00d73 pattern recommended). The seven separate GND_ signal pins must each connect to the board ground plane independently \u2014 do NOT route GND_ pins directly to EP; the ground plane ties them together.",
      "ambiguous": false,
      "datasheet_page": 11
    }
  ],
  "required_passives": [
    {
      "type": "capacitor",
      "value": "100nF ceramic 0402",
      "placement": "Each VCC pin (VCC_CP p6, VCC_PLL p10, VCC_RF p16, VCC_VCO p17, VCC_DIG p28, VCC_SD p32) to adjacent GND",
      "reason": "High-frequency supply decoupling \u2014 one per VCC pin, placed as close as possible. One dedicated via per cap to ground plane.",
      "datasheet_page": 10
    },
    {
      "type": "capacitor",
      "value": "1\u00b5F ceramic",
      "placement": "Each VCC pin (VCC_CP, VCC_PLL, VCC_RF, VCC_VCO, VCC_DIG, VCC_SD) to GND",
      "reason": "Bulk supply decoupling. Star routing from central VCC node recommended to minimise coupling between IC sections.",
      "datasheet_page": 10
    },
    {
      "type": "capacitor",
      "value": "1\u00b5F",
      "placement": "NOISE_FILT (pin 19) to GND",
      "reason": "VCO noise decoupling \u2014 mandatory. Omitting this cap degrades VCO phase noise significantly.",
      "datasheet_page": 11
    },
    {
      "type": "capacitor",
      "value": "1\u00b5F",
      "placement": "BIAS_FILT (pin 23) to GND",
      "reason": "VCO bias filter \u2014 mandatory. Keep trace short.",
      "datasheet_page": 11
    },
    {
      "type": "capacitor",
      "value": "1\u00b5F",
      "placement": "REG (pin 24) to GND",
      "reason": "Internal reference voltage compensation \u2014 mandatory.",
      "datasheet_page": 11
    },
    {
      "type": "capacitor",
      "value": "100nF",
      "placement": "REF_IN (pin 29) AC-coupling to reference oscillator",
      "reason": "REF_IN has an internal bias at VCC_DIG/2. Must be AC-coupled to the external reference source.",
      "datasheet_page": 11
    },
    {
      "type": "resistor",
      "value": "5.1k\u03a9 (range: 2.7k\u03a9\u201310k\u03a9)",
      "placement": "RSET (pin 22) to GND",
      "reason": "Sets minimum CP current: ICP_min = 1.63V/RSET. 5.1k\u03a9 \u2192 0.32mA min / 5.12mA max. Do not omit.",
      "datasheet_page": 11
    },
    {
      "type": "inductor",
      "value": "27nH RF choke",
      "placement": "VCC_RF (pin 16) to each of RFOUTA_P (12), RFOUTA_N (13), RFOUTB_P (14), RFOUTB_N (15) \u2014 one choke per output pin",
      "reason": "Open-collector RF output bias. Provides DC path to supply while blocking RF. Measured with 27nH to VCC_RF into 50\u03a9 load per datasheet Note 4.",
      "datasheet_page": 16
    },
    {
      "type": "resistor",
      "value": "50\u03a9",
      "placement": "Each active RF output pin to GND (load); each unused RF output to GND (termination)",
      "reason": "RF output load/termination. Unused outputs must still be terminated: 27nH choke to VCC_RF + 50\u03a9 to GND.",
      "datasheet_page": 16
    },
    {
      "type": "network",
      "value": "Loop filter: C3=820pF, R3=250\u03a9, C1=0.012\u00b5F, R2=120\u03a9, C2=0.1\u00b5F (40kHz BW at fPFD=25MHz)",
      "placement": "CP_OUT (pin 7) \u2192 C3 to GND, then R3, then junction \u2192 R2 \u2192 C2 to GND; junction also \u2192 TUNE (pin 20). C1 in parallel with R2.",
      "reason": "PLL loop filter. Values from EV kit for 40kHz loop bandwidth at fREF=50MHz, fPFD=25MHz. Adjust with EE-Sim tool for target BW.",
      "datasheet_page": 9
    }
  ],
  "application_circuits": [
    {
      "name": "Typical MAX2870 PLL Frequency Synthesiser (from datasheet p.23)",
      "description": "Three separate LDOs supply VCC_RF, VCC_DIG, and VCC_PLL for best performance. All VCC_ pins (CP, PLL, RF, VCO, DIG, SD) decoupled with 100nF + 1\u00b5F close to each pin. REF_IN AC-coupled via 100nF to external reference oscillator. CP_OUT drives loop filter (C3=820pF, R3=250\u03a9, C1=0.012\u00b5F, R2A=120\u03a9, C2=0.1\u00b5F) with output going to TUNE. RFOUTA_P/N and RFOUTB_P/N each biased via 27nH choke from VCC_RF, AC-coupled to 50\u03a9 loads. NOISE_FILT, BIAS_FILT, REG each with 1\u00b5F cap to GND. RSET with 5.1k\u03a9 to GND. CLK/DATA/LE driven by SPI master. CE and RFOUT_EN pulled high for normal operation. LD and MUX_OUT connected to MCU GPIOs. EP soldered to ground plane with via array.",
      "page": 23
    }
  ],
  "common_mistakes": [
    "GND_CP (8), GND_PLL (9), GND_RF (11), GND_VCO (18), GND_TUNE (21), GND_DIG (27), GND_SD (31) must NOT be routed directly to the exposed paddle (EP) \u2014 each pin routes independently to the board ground plane; the ground plane then ties them all and EP together",
    "TUNE (pin 20) must NOT connect directly to CP_OUT (pin 7) \u2014 TUNE is the output of the loop filter network that sits between CP_OUT and TUNE",
    "After every power-up or CE de-assertion, registers must be programmed TWICE with a \u226520ms pause: first pass enables the device, second pass starts VCO autoselection",
    "Register programming order is mandatory: 5 \u2192 4 \u2192 3 \u2192 2 \u2192 1 \u2192 0 (repeat both times). Writing out of order causes undefined device state",
    "Keep RFOUT disabled during the init sequence (Reg4 bits 4 and 8 = 0); enable outputs using RFA_EN/RFB_EN only after the second complete programming pass",
    "NOISE_FILT (19), BIAS_FILT (23), and REG (24) MUST each have a 1\u00b5F cap to GND \u2014 leaving any of them unconnected causes VCO instability and severely degraded phase noise",
    "RF outputs are open-collector \u2014 they require a 27nH RF choke to VCC_RF for DC bias. Never connect directly to a supply rail or to GND without a choke",
    "Unused RF outputs must still be terminated: 27nH choke to VCC_RF + 50\u03a9 to GND. Do not leave floating",
    "REF_IN MUST be AC-coupled \u2014 it has an internal DC bias at VCC_DIG/2 that DC-coupled sources will disturb",
    "VCC_VCO decoupling caps must connect to the analog ground plane for best phase noise \u2014 using digital ground reduces performance",
    "RSET must have a resistor (2.7k\u03a9\u201310k\u03a9) to GND \u2014 do not leave floating or tie directly to GND",
    "For fractional-N mode, set CPL=01 (Reg1 bits 30:29) and CPOC=0 (Reg1 bit31); for integer-N mode, set CPL=00 and CPOC=1 for better in-band phase noise. Swapping these settings degrades performance",
    "Analog lock detect (LD=10 in Reg5) requires an external 10k\u03a9 pull-up to VCC on the LD pin \u2014 the output is open-drain in this mode",
    "VCO tune voltage compliance range is 0.5V\u20132.5V; ADC codes 000 (VTUNE<0.5V) and 111 (VTUNE>2.5V) indicate out-of-lock even if digital LD shows locked"
  ],
  "notes": "Part number for ordering: MAX2870ETJ+ (TQFN-EP, \u221240\u00b0C to +85\u00b0C, RoHS). Register 6 is read-only and accessible via MUX_OUT when MUX[3:0]=1100; contains POR status, VCO autoselect result (V[5:0]), and VTUNE ADC reading (ADC[2:0]). Phase shift mode available after lock: set P to desired increment \u00d7 M/360, enable with CDM=10, then reset CDM=00. Low-spur modes available via SDN bits (Reg2 bits 30:29): SDN=00 (low-noise), SDN=10 (low-spur mode 1), SDN=11 (low-spur mode 2). VCO manual selection (VAS_SHDN=1) reduces re-lock time by ~200\u00b5s by skipping autoselect; requires pre-built VCO lookup table.",

## All pins (33)
  NUM   NAME                 TYPE         DESCRIPTION
     1  CLK                  input        Serial Clock Input. Data is latched into the 32-bit shift register on the rising edge of CLK.
     2  DATA                 input        Serial Data Input. Data loaded MSB first. The 3 LSBs identify the register address.
     3  LE                   input        Load Enable Input. Rising edge of LE latches the shift register contents into the selected register.
     4  CE                   input        Chip Enable. Logic-low powers down the device; charge pump becomes high impedance. In low-power mode all blocks except SPI are off.
     5  SW                   passive      Fast-Lock Switch. Connect to loop filter when using fast-lock mode. No connect in normal mode.
     6  VCC_CP               power        Power Supply for Charge Pump.
     7  CP_OUT               output       Charge-Pump Output. Current-output node that drives the external loop filter.
     8  GND_CP               gnd          Ground for Charge Pump.
     9  GND_PLL              gnd          Ground for PLL.
    10  VCC_PLL              power        Power Supply for PLL.
    11  GND_RF               gnd          Ground for RF Outputs.
    12  RFOUTA_P             output       Open Collector Positive RF Output A. Differential positive output. Fundamental VCO ÷ DIVA.
    13  RFOUTA_N             output       Open Collector Negative RF Output A. Differential negative output.
    14  RFOUTB_P             output       Open Collector Positive RF Output B. Differential positive output. BDIV=0: same frequency as RFOUTA; BDIV=1: VCO fundamental frequency.
    15  RFOUTB_N             output       Open Collector Negative RF Output B. Differential negative output.
    16  VCC_RF               power        Power Supply for RF Output and Dividers. Also serves as DC bias supply for open-collector RF outputs via 27nH chokes.
    17  VCC_VCO              power        VCO Power Supply.
    18  GND_VCO              gnd          Ground for VCO.
    19  NOISE_FILT           passive      VCO Noise Decoupling. Internal VCO bias node that must be externally decoupled.
    20  TUNE                 input        VCO Control Voltage Input. Driven by the output of the external PLL loop filter. Compliance range: 0.5V to 2.5V.
    21  GND_TUNE             gnd          Ground for VCO Control Input.
    22  RSET                 passive      Charge-Pump Current Range Input. External resistor to GND sets the minimum CP current: ICP = 1.63V/RSET × (1 + CP[3:0]).
    23  BIAS_FILT            passive      VCO Bias Decoupling. Internal VCO bias filter node.
    24  REG                  passive      Reference Voltage Compensation. Internal bandgap/reference decoupling node.
    25  LD                   output       Lock Detect Output. Behaviour set by LD[1:0] bits (Reg5 bits 23:22): 01=digital lock detect, 10=analog lock detect (open-drain), 00=always low, 11=always high.
    26  RFOUT_EN             input        RF Output Enable (hardware). Logic-low disables both RFOUTA and RFOUTB regardless of register settings.
    27  GND_DIG              gnd          Ground for Digital Circuitry.
    28  VCC_DIG              power        Power Supply for Digital Circuitry. Also provides the internal bias level VCC_DIG/2 for the REF_IN input.
    29  REF_IN               input        Reference Frequency Input. High-impedance CMOS inverter with internal bias at VCC_DIG/2. Input range 10–200MHz (or 10–100MHz with reference doubler DBR=1). In shutdown, input is high-impedance.
    30  MUX_OUT              output       Multiplexed Output / Serial Data Out. Function selected by MUX[3:0] (Reg2 bits 28:26 + Reg5 bit18 MSB). Options: high-Z, D_VDD, D_GND, R-divider output, N-divider/2, analog lock detect, digital lock detect, or Register-6 readback (MUX=1100).
    31  GND_SD               gnd          Ground for Sigma-Delta Modulator.
    32  VCC_SD               power        Power Supply for Sigma-Delta Modulator.
    EP  EP                   gnd          Exposed Pad. Thermal and ground pad on the bottom of the package. Must be soldered to PCB ground plane for proper electrical and thermal performance.

"MAX2870 23.5MHz to 6000MHz Fractional/\nInteger-N Synthesizer/VCO\nGeneral Description Benefits and Features\nThe MAX2870 is an ultra-wideband phase-locked U Integer and Fractional-N Modes\nloop (PLL) with integrated voltage control oscillators\nU Manual or Automatic VCO Selection\n(VCOs) capable of operating in both integer-N and\nfractional-N modes. When combined with an external U 3000MHz to 6000MHz Fundamental VCO\nreference oscillator and loop filter, the MAX2870 is U Output Binary Buffers/Dividers for Extended\na high-performance frequency synthesizer capable Frequency Range\nof synthesizing frequencies from 23.5MHz to 6.0GHz\n• 1/2/4/8/16/32/64/128\nwhile maintaining superior phase noise and spurious\nperformance. • 23.5MHz to 6000MHz\nThe ultra-wide frequency range is achieved with the U High-Performance PFD\nhelp of multiple integrated VCOs covering 3000MHz to • 105MHz in Integer-N Mode\n6000MHz, and output dividers ranging from 1 to 128.\n• 50MHz in Fractional-N Mode\nThe device also provides dual differential output drivers,\nwhich can be independently programmable to deliver U ReferenceFrequencyUpto200MHz\n-4dBm to +5dBm output power. Both outputs can be U Operates from +3.0V to +3.6V Supply\nmuted by either software or hardware control.\nU Dual Programmable Outputs\nThe MAX2870 is controlled by a 4-wire serial interface and\nis compatible with 1.8V control logic. The device is available • -4dBm to +5dBm\nin a lead-free, RoHS-compliant, 5mm x 5mm, 32-pin TQFN U Analog and Digital Lock Detect Indicators\npackage, and operates over an extended -40NC to +85NC\nU Hardware and Software Shutdown Control\ntemperaturerange.\nU Compatible with 1.8V Control Logic\nApplications\nWireless Infrastructure PMAR/LMAR/Public Ordering Information appears at end of data sheet.\nTest and Measurement Safety Radio\nSatellite Communications Clock Generation Typical Application Circuit appears at end of data sheet.\nWireless LANs/CATV\nFunctional Diagram\nMUX_OUT\nMUX\nMAX2870\nLD\nLOCK DETECT\nREF_IN MUX R COUNTER DIVIDE-BY-2 MUX CHARGE CP_OUT\nX2 PUMP\nCP_GND\nCLK\nTUNE\nDATA SPI AND\nLE REGISTERS\nVCO\nINTEGER FRAC MODULUS\nRFOUTA_P\nDIV-BY- DIV-BY-\n1/2/4/8/16 1/2/4/8 RFOUTA_N\nRFOUT_EN\nMAIN\nMODULATOR RFOUTB_P\nMUX RFOUTB_N\nN COUNTER\nMUX\n19-6250;Rev6;4/17\nMAX2870 23.5MHz to 6000MHz Fractional/\nInteger-N Synthesizer/VCO\nAbsolute Maximum Ratings\nVCC_to GND_ .......................................................-0.3V to +3.9V Junction Temperature .....................................................+150NC\nAll Other Pins to GND_ ..............................-0.3V to VCC_ + 0.3V Operating Temperature Range ..........................-40NC to +85NC\nContinuous Power Dissipation (TA = +70NC) Storage Temperature Range ............................-65NC to +150NC\nTQFN-EP Multilayer Board Lead Temperature (soldering, 10s) .............................. +300NC\n(derate 34.5mW/NC above +70NC) .........................2758.6mW Soldering temperature (reflow) .......................................+260NC\nStresses beyond those listed under “Absolute Maximum Ratings” may cause permanent damage to the device.
